chapter_title.en: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them

text: 692 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «كَأَنِّي أَنْظُرُ إِلَى دَيْرَانِيٍّ فِي مَسْجِدِ اَلْكُوفَةِ فِي دَيْرٍ لَهُ فِيمَا بَيْنَ اَلزَّاوِيَةِ وَ اَلْمِنْبَرِ فِيهِ سَبْعُ نَخَلاَتٍ وَ هُوَ مُشْرِفٌ مِنْ دَيْرِهِ عَلَى نُوحٍ يُكَلِّمُهُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.692 - Imam (as) said:
"It is as if I can see a monk in the Mosque of Kufa, in his monastery, between the corner and the pulpit, where there are seven date palms, watching and conversing with Noah (as) from his elevated position."
